turns.content: Review my meeting notes from yesterday

Jm, I wasn't able to locate any meeting notes from yesterday (2026-02-11). Here's what I checked:

- Current working directory — no matching files
- Obsidian vault locations — not found
- System-wide Spotlight search — no meeting note files

A few possibilities:

1. **Notes are in a different app** — Google Docs, Notion, Apple Notes, etc.
2. **Notes weren't saved yet** — still in your head or on paper
3. **Different naming convention** — if you can tell me the filename or location, I'll pull them up

🗣️ Kaya: No meeting notes found from yesterday. Where are they stored — Obsidian, Docs, or elsewhere?

tool_calls.name: Bash

params.description: Search Obsidian vault and notes dirs for recent meeting notes

tool_calls.result:
---
(eval):1: no matches found: /Users/[user]/Documents/Obsidian*
(eval):1: no matches found: /Users/[user]/Documents/Obsidian*

params.command: mdfind "kMDItemDisplayName == '*meeting*' && kMDItemContentModificationDate >= '$time.today(-2)'" 2>/dev/null | head -20; echo "==="; mdfind "kMDItemDisplayName == '*2026-02-11*'" 2>/dev/null | head -20
params.description: Spotlight search for meeting notes modified in last 2 days
